misleading_claim: Aliasing is the strongest optimizer enabler
why_misleading: Current corpus is selection-biased toward aliasing; many areas lack confirmed evidence.
preferred_wording: Say: non-interference is well-attested in selected sources.

misleading_claim: Rust ownership automatically maps to LLVM noalias everywhere
why_misleading: Unsafe, interior mutability, raw pointers, shared references, callbacks, and compiler model details complicate lowering.
preferred_wording: Say: Rust-style exclusivity is evidence for language-level non-interference, subject to contract details.

why_misleading: HotSpot escape analysis can eliminate some allocations/locks; costs depend on barriers, safepoints, allocation patterns, and runtime implementation.
preferred_wording: Say: managed runtimes can recover some performance conditionally, unlike source-level guarantees.

why_misleading: EH cost depends on source semantics, ABI, representation, hot/cold paths, and backend constraints; two broad LLVM EH claims were refuted.
preferred_wording: Say: exception representation affects optimization and needs precise evidence.

why_misleading: Metadata is sound only if frontend semantics/proofs are sound and preserved.
preferred_wording: Say: IR mechanisms are conditional carriers of source facts.
